quality.rationale: 通过静态时空度规结构与联络计算导出Ricci张量分量矛盾，并辅以Raychaudhuri方程严格证明，逻辑链条完整且揭示宇宙动力学本质限制

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s19_19.36.md]Problem 19.36: Prove that no solution of the Einstein equations for a pressureless fluid is static. Do not assume isotropy or homogeneity. (The difficulty of this problem depends on the definition of "static.") Easy case: Take static to mean time invariant and time reversible, the first definition of "static" in Problem 10.8. Harder case: Use the second definition in Problem 10.8.)## Solution
origin.answer: 不存在压力为零流体的静态解。该假设导出 ρ + 3p = 0 或 Raychaudhuri 方程 0 = -2σ² - 1/3θ² - 4πρ，均与正能量条件 ρ>0, p=0 矛盾。
origin.solution: Solutions 19.36 and 19.37. \"Easy\" cases: Since the metric is time invariant and reversible, $\mathbf{g}_{0\mathbf{i}} = 0$ and it has the form$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {g}_{0 0} (\mathbf {x}^{\mathrm {i}}) \mathrm {d t}^{2} + \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {i j}} (\mathbf {x}^{\mathrm {k}}) \mathrm {d x}^{\mathrm {i}} \mathrm {d x}^{\mathrm {j}}.$$If it is homogeneous (Problem 19.37), clocks must tick at the same rate everywhere, so $\mathbf{g}_{00} =$ constant; if it is inhomogeneous but pressureless (Problem 19.36), it is possible to rescale the time coordinate locally to make $\mathbf{g}_{00} =$ constant. (This was shown in Problem 16.25.) The standard formula for $\Gamma_{\mu \nu \sigma}$ now shows by inspection that the only nonvanishing components are $\Gamma_{ijk}$ . (Any 0 component is either a time derivative of a component $\mathbf{g}_{0j}$ or a derivative of $\mathbf{g}_{00}$ .) Now $\mathbf{R}_{00}$ can be computed from the standard formula involving the $\Gamma$ 's; since every term contains a zero index on a $\Gamma$ , it follows that $\mathbf{R}_{00} = 0$ and hence$$\mathbf {T}_{\hat {0}}^{\hat {0}} - \frac {1}{2} \mathbf {T}_{\hat {\alpha}}^{\hat {a}} = 0$$and hence $\rho + 3\mathfrak{p} = 0$ . But this is impossible for a pressureless fluid, or for a physically reasonable perfect fluid (with positive $\mathfrak{p}$ and $\rho$ ).\"Harder\" cases: Let $\pmb{\xi}$ be the time-symmetry Killing vector and $\mathbf{u}$ be the 4-velocity of the fluid. Since $\mathbf{u} \propto \pmb{\xi}$ (Problem 13.9) and $\pmb{\xi}$ is hypersurface-orthogonal, $\mathbf{u}$ is hypersurface-orthogonal and hence $\omega_{\alpha \beta} = 0$ . In the Raychaudhuri equation (Problem 14.10)$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} r} \propto \frac {\partial \theta}{\partial t} = 0$$where $\pmb {\xi} = \partial /\partial t$ . Thus the Raychaudhuri equation becomes$$0 = \mathrm {a}_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - 8 \pi \left(\mathrm {T}_{\alpha \beta} - \frac {1}{2} \mathrm {g}_{\alpha \beta} \mathrm {T}\right) \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta}. \tag {1}$$For $p = 0$ (Problem 19.36) we have $a = 0$ from the Euler equation (Problem 14.3) and Equation (1) becomes$$0 = - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - 4 \pi \rho$$which has no solution for positive $\rho$ .When there is pressure, then in general a is nonzero; in fact,$$\mathbf {a} = \nabla \log | \boldsymbol {\xi} \cdot \boldsymbol {\xi} |^{\frac {1}{2}}.$$If we assume homogeneity (Problem 19.37), then $\mathbf{a} = 0$ and$$0 = - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - 4 \pi (\rho + 3 p),$$which again has no solution for $\rho + 3\mathfrak{p} > 0 .$
